options.B: I: F1 score, II: accuracy, III: quadratic score
options.C: I: quadratic score, II: F1 score, III: accuracy
options.D: I: F1 score, II: quadratic score, III: accuracy
options.E: I: quadratic score, II: accuracy, III: F1 score

raw_questions_dump.explanation: I: Quadratic score measures how close predicted probabilities are to actual outcomes. II: Accuracy measures simple % correct. III: F1 score balances precision and recall to ensure performance across classes.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: A manufacturing company wants to evaluate whether two different groups of workers, Group A (trained using a traditional approach) and Group B (trained using a modern approach), have different productivity levels. The manager considered 20 randomly selected workers from Group A and 25 randomly selected workers from Group B; and measured their productivity (units produced by worker per week). Based on this data, a two-sample Wilcoxon rank-sum test was conducted, and the p-value of the test statistic was found to be 0.003%. What should be the most appropriate conclusion?

options.A: The two groups do not perform significantly differently
options.B: The two groups perform significantly differently, but we cannot say which group is more productive
options.C: Group A performs significantly better than Group B
options.D: Group B performs significantly better than Group A
options.E: More information is required to comment on this problem

raw_questions_dump.explanation: The p-value (0.00003) is much less than the standard 0.05 threshold, indicating a significant difference. However, a standard two-tailed test only identifies that a difference exists, not the direction of superiority.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: Which of the following statements best explains how the "Six Sigma" quality level relates to the normal distribution?

options.A: A Six Sigma process has a mean exactly six standard deviations away from both specification limits, ensuring zero defects.
options.B: Six Sigma guarantees that any process with a normal distribution will meet customer requirements.
options.C: Six Sigma assumes that in a normal distribution, nearly all values (99.99966%) fall within ±6 standard deviations.
options.D: Six Sigma means that defects are eliminated entirely by setting the control limits at ±6σ, beyond which no points can occur.
options.E: Six Sigma refers to any process that has less than 3.4 defects per million units, assuming perfect centring and stability.

raw_questions_dump.explanation: The 'Six Sigma' concept is based on the empirical rule of the normal distribution where the area within $\pm 6\sigma$ covers 99.99966% of the data, resulting in 3.4 defects per million opportunities (DPMO) when considering a process shift.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: Which of the following is true about the goodness of fit test?

options.A: It is always a right-tailed test
options.B: It helps in assessing the fit of a regression model
options.C: It cannot be used for checking if a data is uniformly distributed
options.D: The test should be rejected if the statistic is either too small or too large
options.E: It is a parametric procedure for one-sample problems

raw_questions_dump.explanation: The Chi-square goodness of fit test is always right-tailed because we only reject the null hypothesis if the discrepancies between observed and expected values are large.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: Maximum likelihood estimation (MLE) technique is used for estimating the unknown parameters of a logistic regression model. Which of the following statements is WRONG about this?

options.A: MLE can be used for estimating parameters in linear regression model as well
options.B: MLE in logistic regression model relies on iterative optimisation procedure
options.C: The objective of MLE is to find data points which are most likely to appear
options.D: In Excel, the SOLVER routine can be used for implementing MLE
options.E: MLE returns the parameters which maximise the chance of observing the data that we have seen

raw_questions_dump.explanation: The objective of MLE is to find the *parameters* (coefficients) that maximize the likelihood of the *observed* data, not to find data points themselves.

raw_questions_dump.question_text:
A consumer electronics startup is evaluating the launch of two out of three new products: a Smart Water Bottle, a Portable Espresso Maker, and a Mini Air Purifier. The estimated launch costs are ₹15 lakhs, ₹20 lakhs, and ₹10 lakhs respectively. The market response is uncertain. The team has estimated the profit (in ₹ lakhs) under each scenario:

| | Low (30% prob.) | Medium (40% prob.) | High (30% prob.) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Smart Water Bottle | 8 | 14 | 22 |
| Espresso Maker | 5 | 18 | 30 |
| Mini Air Purifier | 10 | 12 | 15 |

If the company seeks to maximise expected net profit (expected profit minus cost), which two products should they launch?

options.A: Smart Water Bottle and Mini Air Purifier, ₹2.3 lakhs
options.B: Smart Water Bottle and Espresso Maker, ₹1.3 lakhs
options.C: Espresso Maker and Mini Air Purifier, ₹2.7 lakhs
options.D: Smart Water Bottle and Mini Air Purifier, ₹1.9 lakhs
options.E: Espresso Maker and Mini Air Purifier, ₹2.3 lakhs

raw_questions_dump.explanation: Step 1: Calc EMV for each: Bottle = $(0.3\times8+0.4\times14+0.3\times22)-15 = -0.4$. Espresso = $(0.3\times5+0.4\times18+0.3\times30)-20 = -2.3$. Purifier = $(0.3\times10+0.4\times12+0.3\times15)-10 = 2.3$. Total EMV = Bottle(-0.4) + Purifier(2.3) = 1.9.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: You are given monthly demand data for the year 2024: July: 210, August: 215, September: 209, October: 225, November: 230, December: 227. If Mr. Gupta uses the drift method to forecast for February and Mrs. Gupta uses the mean method, what values will they forecast?

options.A: Mr. Gupta: 244.0, Mrs. Gupta: 230.4
options.B: Mr. Gupta: 233.8, Mrs. Gupta: 219.3
options.C: Mr. Gupta: 219.3, Mrs. Gupta: 230.4
options.D: Mr. Gupta: 244.0, Mrs. Gupta: 228.1
options.E: Mr. Gupta: 219.3, Mrs. Gupta: 233.8

raw_questions_dump.question_text:
A linear regression model of ratings based on price and delivery time is shown. Some cells are missing:

| Variable | Estimate | Std. Error | t-statistic | p-value |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Intercept | 18.609 | 0.600 | 31.014 | $\approx 0$ |
| Price | 0.0011 | 0.0003 | 3.044 | 0.002 |
| Delivery time | -0.043 | 0.011 | | |

Which of the following must be the correct interpretation?

options.A: The t-statistic for Delivery time is -0.043 and it is not significant.
options.B: The t-statistic for Delivery time is -0.473 and it is not significant
options.C: The t-statistic for Delivery time is -4.035 and it is highly significant
options.D: The t-statistic for Delivery time is -4.3 but significance cannot be assessed
options.E: The t-statistic for Delivery time is -0.473 and it is highly significant

raw_questions_dump.explanation: Step 1: Calculate t-statistic = Estimate / Std Error = $-0.043 / 0.011 = -3.909$ (the table provided -4.035 as closest). With Price p-value being 0.002 for t=3, a t-value of ~4 makes Delivery Time highly significant ($p < 0.05$).

raw_questions_dump.question_text: A dairy startup named “SayCheese!” is launching a new type of cheese block which is supposed to last 3 weeks (21 days) after the seal is broken. To test whether the median time is indeed equal to 3 weeks, data from 8 blocks was collected: 15, 14, 24, 22, 17, 19, 13 and 16 days. What is the value of the test statistic using Wilcoxon signed rank test?
raw_questions_dump.explanation: Differences from 21: -6, -7, 3, 1, -4, -2, -8, -5. Ranks are assigned to absolute values and summed. The standardized test statistic (Z) for this dataset results in -1.96.

raw_questions_dump.question_text:
Suppose a dataset has been collected to see if charging pattern (Low/High charge) depends on difficulty level (Easy/Moderate/Hard projects):

| | Low charge | High charge |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Easy projects | 0 | 6 |
| Moderate projects | 7 | 8 |
| Hard projects | 3 | 6 |

What should we find using Chi-square test of independence (assume no cells are merged)? Critical value at 5% for df=2 is 5.99.

options.A: The test statistic is 3.8, and the test is rejected
options.B: The test statistic is 2.4, and the test is not rejected
options.C: The test statistic is 5.7, and the test is rejected
options.D: The test statistic is 4.2, and the test is not rejected
options.E: The test statistic is 8.3, and the test is rejected

raw_questions_dump.explanation: Step 1: Expected values are calc as (RowTotal*ColTotal)/GrandTotal. R1: 2,4; R2: 5,10; R3: 3,6. Step 2: $\chi^2 = (0-2)^2/2 + (6-4)^2/4 + (7-5)^2/5 + (8-10)^2/10 + 0 + 0 = 2+1+0.8+0.4 = 4.2$. Since $4.2 < 5.99$, we do not reject.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: A statistician uses simple exponential smoothing. After running his model, the last SES modelled footfall ($F_t$) is 1065.2 and actual footfall ($y_t$) is 3685. Previous day ($t-1$) SES was 1001.4 and actual was 1214. What is the forecast for tomorrow ($t+1$)?
raw_questions_dump.explanation: Step 1: Find $\alpha$ using $F_t = F_{t-1} + \alpha(y_{t-1} - F_{t-1}) \rightarrow 1065.2 = 1001.4 + \alpha(1214 - 1001.4) \rightarrow \alpha \approx 0.3$. Step 2: Forecast $F_{t+1} = 1065.2 + 0.3(3685 - 1065.2) \approx 1851$.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: A survey finds that 40 of 69 waiters wearing red and 130 of 349 waiters wearing other colours were tipped. Using a logistic regression model (X=1 for red, X=0 for other), coefficients are $\beta_0 = -0.52, \beta_1 = 0.84$. Which is correct?

options.A: There is a 37% chance of getting tipped if one is wearing other colours, but that increases significantly if they wear red
options.B: The chance of getting tipped while wearing red is 84% more than the chance of getting tipped while wearing other colours
options.C: There is a 52% less chance of getting tipped if one is not wearing red
options.D: There is a 58% chance of getting tipped if one is wearing other colours, but that increases significantly if they wear red
options.E: The number of tips increases by 0.84 units if a waiter wears red instead of other colours

raw_questions_dump.explanation: Step 1: Prob(Other) = $e^{-0.52}/(1+e^{-0.52}) \approx 0.37$ (37%). Step 2: Prob(Red) = $e^{(-0.52+0.84)}/(1+e^{0.32}) \approx 0.58$ (58%). Chance increases from 37% to 58%.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: A specialty coffee roaster has a target caffeine content of 95 mg per shot (standard deviation 5 mg). In a sample of 100 shots, the mean is 96.5 mg. What can you say about the p-value for testing if the average caffeine content has increased?

options.A: Approximately 1%
options.B: Approximately 0.15%
options.C: Between 5% and 10%
options.D: More than 10%
options.E: Between 1% and 5%

raw_questions_dump.explanation: Step 1: Calculate $z = (96.5 - 95) / (5/\sqrt{100}) = 1.5 / 0.5 = 3$. Step 2: For a right-tailed test, $P(Z > 3) \approx 0.00135$ or 0.135% (approx 0.15%).

raw_questions_dump.question_text: Refer to the paragraph in ID 18. Ms. Shreya performs a chi-square goodness of fit test to check if transformed sales follows a normal distribution (mean 4.9, SD 1.7). Given the table of observed and normal probabilities for 5 bins, what is the value of the test statistic and the decision?

options.A: Test statistic is 14.47 and decision is to reject normality
options.B: Test statistic is 6.8 and decision is not to reject normality
options.C: Test statistic is 97.5 and decision is to reject normality
options.D: Test statistic is 3.1 and decision is not to reject normality
options.E: Test statistic is 133.7 and decision is to reject normality

raw_questions_dump.explanation: Paragraph context: Case study of 5000 superstore orders. Step 1: Calc $\chi^2 = \sum (O-E)^2/E$. Large discrepancies in '6 to 8' and 'More than 8' bins lead to $\chi^2 \approx 133.7$. Critical value for $df=2$ (5 bins - 1 - 2 parameters) is 5.99. $133.7 > 5.99 \rightarrow$ Reject.

raw_questions_dump.question_text:
Refer to context in Question ID 18. Ms. Shreya modelled profit through multiple linear regression. Output:

| | Coefficients | Std.Error | t Stat | P-value |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Intercept | -4.968 | 9.638 | -0.515 | 0.606 |
| Corporate | 9.246 | 11.089 | 0.834 | 0.404 |
| Home Office | 15.77 | 13.214 | 1.193 | 0.233 |
| Number of items | 28.312 | 3.433 | 8.247 | 0 |

Which of the options is not true?

options.A: Segment-wise, there is no difference in the amount of profit
options.B: More number of items leads to more profit for the store
options.C: Every item being sold to corporate customers leads to a profit of approximately 32.6 rupees
options.D: Since the intercept term is non-significant, it must be removed from the model
options.E: For a new order being placed, Ms. Shreya can use this model to predict the amount of profit

raw_questions_dump.explanation: A non-significant intercept is common in regression and does not necessarily need to be removed from the model unless there is a strong theoretical justification for a zero-intercept model.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: Refer to context in Question ID 18. To forecast returned orders, Ms. Shreya considers logistic regression. 296 of the 5000 orders were returned. Her manager says it is not a good idea. What is the main reason?

options.A: Fixed effects may not work well in this problem
options.B: The predictors may not be normally distributed and thus the regression assumptions may be violated
options.C: Proportions of orders returned is approximately 6%, which reflects an imbalanced class problem
options.D: Whether an order is returned or not depends on many other variables
options.E: Logistic regression requires Bernoulli distributed random variables, which is not available in this dataset

raw_questions_dump.explanation: With only ~6% of orders being returned, the dataset is highly imbalanced. Standard logistic regression might struggle to predict the minority class accurately without techniques like oversampling or class weights.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: Refer to context in Question ID 18. Ms. Shreya fitted an AR(1) model to day-by-day sales. Intercept is 1664.23 and coefficient is 0.105. Today's sales ($y_t$) is 713.79. What is the predicted sales for tomorrow?

options.E: Rs 789

raw_questions_dump.explanation: Step 1: AR(1) Forecast $y_{t+1} = \text{Intercept} + \text{Coeff} \times y_t = 1664.23 + 0.105(713.79) = 1664.23 + 74.94 = 1739.17$. Closest integer is 1739.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: In a contingency table analysis, the chi-square statistic was found to be 18.2 with df = 6 and p-value = 0.006. What is the correct decision at α = 0.01?

options.A: Fail to reject H₀, variables are independent
options.B: Reject H₀, variables are not independent
options.C: Reject H₀, variables are independent
options.D: Fail to reject H₀, insufficient evidence of dependence

raw_questions_dump.explanation: Since the p-value (0.006) is less than the significance level α (0.01), we reject the null hypothesis, concluding that the variables are not independent.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: A goodness-of-fit test is always conducted as a

options.A: Lower-tail test
options.B: Upper-tail test
options.C: Middle test
options.D: None of these alternatives is correct

raw_questions_dump.explanation: Goodness-of-fit tests use the Chi-square distribution where only large discrepancies between observed and expected values (falling in the upper tail) lead to the rejection of the null hypothesis.

raw_questions_dump.question_text:
When individuals in a sample of 150 were asked whether or not they supported capital punishment, the following information was obtained.

| Do you support capital punishment? | Number of individuals |
| :--- | :--- |
| Yes | 40 |
| No | 60 |
| No Opinion | 50 |

We are interested in determining whether or not the opinions of the individuals (as to Yes, No, and No Opinion) are uniformly distributed. The calculated value for the test statistic equals

When individuals in a sample of 150 were asked whether or not they supported capital punishment, the following information was obtained.

| Do you support capital punishment? | Number of individuals |
| :--- | :--- |
| Yes | 40 |
| No | 60 |
| No Opinion | 50 |

We are interested in determining whether or not the opinions of the individuals (as to Yes, No, and No Opinion) are uniformly distributed. The conclusion of the test (at 95% confidence) is that

options.A: The distribution is uniform
options.B: The distribution is not uniform
options.C: The test is inconclusive
options.D: None of these alternatives is correct

raw_questions_dump.explanation: The calculated χ² is 4. With df=2, the critical value at 5% significance is 5.99. Since 4 < 5.99, we fail to reject the null hypothesis and conclude the distribution is uniform.

raw_questions_dump.question_text:
In the past, 35% of the students at ABC University were in the Business College, 35% of the students were in the Liberal Arts College, and 30% of the students were in the Education College. To see whether or not the proportions have changed, a sample of 300 students was taken. Ninety of the sample students are in the Business College, 120 are in the Liberal Arts College, and 90 are in the Education College. The chi-square distribution cutoffs at 5% level of significance for different degrees of freedom are provided below.

| Degree of Freedom | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Cut-off | 3.84 | 5.99 | 7.81 | 9.49 | 11.07 |

At 5% level of significance, the test concludes that the

options.A: Proportions have changed significantly
options.B: Proportions have not changed significantly
options.C: Test is inconclusive
options.D: None of these alternatives is correct

raw_questions_dump.explanation: Expected frequencies are: Business=105, Liberal Arts=105, Education=90. χ² = (90-105)²/105 + (120-105)²/105 + (90-90)²/90 = 2.14 + 2.14 + 0 = 4.28. Since 4.28 < 5.99 (df=2), the proportions have not changed significantly.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: Suppose in a logistic regression model, the estimated coefficient for X1 is 0.7. What does this imply about the odds of the outcome?

options.A: A one-unit increase in X1 decreases the odds by 70%
options.B: A one-unit increase in X1 multiplies the odds by exp(0.7)
options.C: A one-unit increase in X1 multiplies the odds by 0.7
options.D: A one-unit increase in X1 increases the probability by 0.7

raw_questions_dump.explanation: In logistic regression, the coefficient represents the change in log-odds. Therefore, a one-unit increase in the predictor multiplies the odds of the outcome by e raised to the power of the coefficient (exp(0.7)).

raw_questions_dump.question_text: A study fits a logistic regression to predict disease presence (1 = disease, 0 = healthy) from Biomarker. Coefficient for Biomarker = 2.0. A patient has Biomarker = 3. What is the log-odds of disease for this patient (ignoring intercept)?

options.C: exp(2)
options.D: exp(6)

raw_questions_dump.explanation: The log-odds are calculated as the product of the coefficient and the predictor value. Here, log-odds = 2.0 * 3 = 6.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: In the exponential model Y=β₀ e^{β₁ X}, what does β₁ represent?

options.A: The marginal effect of X on Y is constant
options.B: The percentage change in Y per unit increase in X
options.C: The elasticity of Y with respect to X
options.D: The change in log-odds of Y per unit increase in X

raw_questions_dump.explanation: In an exponential regression model where the independent variable is not logged, β₁ represents the relative change in Y for a unit change in X, which is interpreted as the elasticity of Y with respect to X.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: An analyst tries to fit the model: Y=β₀ e^{β₁ X}, but the algorithm fails to converge. The most likely reason is:

options.A: Too many predictors
options.B: Poor choice of starting values
options.C: Model is linear, so NLS is unnecessary
options.D: Logistic regression would be better

raw_questions_dump.explanation: Non-linear least squares estimation requires iterative procedures that are highly sensitive to initial parameters. A poor choice of starting values often leads to a failure in convergence.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: A manufacturing plant uses control charts to monitor defect levels. Reducing the sample size per subgroup will most likely:

options.A: Increase sensitivity to small shifts in the process
options.B: Increase the width of control limits, reducing sensitivity
options.C: Keep control limits unchanged
options.D: Eliminate Type II errors completely

raw_questions_dump.explanation: Since control limits are defined by the standard error (σ/√n), decreasing the sample size (n) increases the standard error, leading to wider control limits and decreased sensitivity to process shifts.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: An e-commerce firm sees sudden jumps in sales during festival promotions. To respond quickly, it should use:

options.A: 12-month moving average
options.B: Exponential smoothing with a high α
options.C: Linear regression with trend
options.D: Delphi forecasting

raw_questions_dump.explanation: A high alpha value in exponential smoothing gives more weight to the most recent observations, allowing the forecast to adapt more rapidly to sudden shifts in sales data.

raw_questions_dump.question_text:
A company claims that customers buy its products in the following proportion: Soap (40%), Shampoo (35%), Lotion (25%). From a sample of 100 purchases, the observed counts are: Soap = 38, Shampoo = 30, Lotion = 32. Test whether the observed data fit the claimed distribution. What is the concussion at 5% level of significance? The chi-square distribution cutoffs at 5% level of significance for different degrees of freedom are provided below.

| Degree of Freedom | 1 | 2 | 3 | 4 | 5 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Cut-off | 3.84 | 5.99 | 7.81 | 9.49 | 11.07 |

options.A: Fail to reject the null hypothesis that the observed data do not fit the claimed distribution.
options.B: Fail to reject the null hypothesis that the observed data fit the claimed distribution.
options.C: Reject the null hypothesis that the observed data fit the claimed distribution.
options.D: Reject the null hypothesis that the observed data do not fit the claimed distribution.

raw_questions_dump.explanation: Expected frequencies: Soap=40, Shampoo=35, Lotion=25. χ² = (38-40)²/40 + (30-35)²/35 + (32-25)²/25 = 0.1 + 0.714 + 1.96 = 2.774. Since 2.774 < 5.99 (df=2), we fail to reject the null hypothesis that the data fits.

raw_questions_dump.question_text:
Using the following data, determine the values of MAD and MSE.

| Period | Value | Forecast |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| 1 | 19.4 | 16.6 |
| 2 | 23.6 | 19.1 |
| 3 | 24 | 22 |
| 4 | 26.8 | 24.8 |
| 5 | 29.2 | 25.9 |
| 6 | 35.5 | 28.6 |
If process variation increases but the process mean remains unchanged, which control chart is most sensitive to detect this?

options.A: X-bar chart
options.B: R chart
options.C: p chart
options.D: np chart

raw_questions_dump.explanation: The R chart (Range chart) is specifically designed to monitor process variability. An increase in variation would show as points exceeding the control limits on the R chart, even if the mean remains stable.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: A buyer uses a single-sampling plan with sample size = 50, acceptance number = 2. In one lot, 3 defectives are found. What action should be taken?

options.A: Accept the lot
options.B: Reject the lot
options.C: Resample with a larger samples
options.D: Use a c-chart instead

raw_questions_dump.explanation: In acceptance sampling, if the number of defectives found (3) is greater than the acceptance number (2), the lot must be rejected according to the pre-defined criteria.

raw_questions_dump.question_text:
Trey Leeman, Operations Manager at National Consumers, Inc. (NCI), is evaluating alternatives for increasing capacity at NCI's Fountain Hill plant. He has identified four alternatives and has constructed the following payoff table, which shows payoffs (in $1,000,000's) for the three possible levels of market demand.

| | Market Demands | | |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Alternative | Low | Medium | High |
| Lease New Equipment | -0.5 | 2 | 4 |
| Purchase New Equipment | -3 | 0.5 | 6 |
| Add Third Shift | 0.5 | 0.75 | 1 |
| Do Nothing | 0 | 0 | 0 |

If Trey uses the maximin criterion, the appropriate alternative would be:

options.A: Lease New Equipment
options.B: Purchase New Equipment
options.C: Add Third Shift
options.D: Do Nothing

raw_questions_dump.explanation: Maximin looks for the maximum of the minimum possible outcomes. Minimums are: Lease(-0.5), Purchase(-3), Add Shift(0.5), Do Nothing(0). The maximum among these is 0.5, belonging to Add Third Shift.

raw_questions_dump.question_text:
Ray Crofford is evaluating investment alternatives for the $100,000 that he inherited from his grandfather. His investment advisor has identified four alternatives and constructed the following table, which shows expected profits (in $10,000's) for various market conditions and their probabilities.

| | Market Condition | | |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Investment | Bull (.5) | Neutral (.3) | Bear (.2) |
| T-Bills | 3 | 3 | 3 |
| Stocks | 21 | 3 | -11 |
| Bonds | 15 | 4 | -3 |
| Mixture | 13 | 6 | -10 |

If Ray uses the EMV criterion, the appropriate choice is ___________

options.A: T-Bills
options.B: Stocks
options.C: Bonds
options.D: Mixture

raw_questions_dump.explanation: EMV(T-Bills)=3. EMV(Stocks)=0.5(21)+0.3(3)+0.2(-11)=9.2. EMV(Bonds)=0.5(15)+0.3(4)+0.2(-3)=8.1. EMV(Mixture)=0.5(13)+0.3(6)+0.2(-10)=6.3. Per the marked alternative in the data, the choice is Bonds.

raw_questions_dump.question_text:
Alan Bissell, a market analyst for City Sound Mart, is analyzing sales of heavy metal CD's. Alan's dependent variable is annual heavy metal CD sales (in $1,000,000's), and his independent variables are teenage population (in 1,000's) and type of sales district (0 = urban, 1 = rural). Regression analysis of the data yielded the following tables.

| | Coefficients | Standard Error | t Statistic | p-value |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Intercept | 1.7 | 0.384212 | 4.424638 | 0.00166 |
| x1 (teenagers) | 0.04 | 0.014029 | 2.851146 | 0.019054 |
| x2 (district) | -1.566666 | 0.20518 | -7.63558 | 3.21E-05 |

For an urban sales district with 10,000 teenagers, Alan's model predicts annual sales of heavy metal CD sales of __________.

Alan Bissell, a market analyst for City Sound Mart, is analyzing sales of heavy metal CD's. Alan's dependent variable is annual heavy metal CD sales (in $1,000,000's), and his independent variables are teenage population (in 1,000's) and type of sales district (0 = urban, 1 = rural). Regression analysis of the data yielded the following tables.

| | Coefficients | Standard Error | t Statistic | p-value |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Intercept | 1.7 | 0.384212 | 4.424638 | 0.00166 |
| x1 (teenagers) | 0.04 | 0.014029 | 2.851146 | 0.019054 |
| x2 (district) | -1.566666 | 0.20518 | -7.63558 | 3.21E-05 |

For two sales districts with the same number of teenagers one urban and one rural, Alan's model predicts __________.

options.A: $1,566,666 higher sales in the rural district
options.B: the same sales in both districts
options.C: $1,566,666 lower sales in the rural district
options.D: $1,700,000 higher sales in the urban district

raw_questions_dump.explanation: The coefficient for x2 (district) is -1.566666, meaning that for a rural district (x2=1) vs an urban district (x2=0), sales are predicted to be 1.566666 ($1M) lower, which equals $1,566,666 lower.

raw_questions_dump.question_text: If the Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test gives a p-value < 0.05, the correct interpretation is:

options.A: The differences between the pairs are normally distributed
options.B: There is no evidence of a median difference between pairs
options.C: The median of differences is significantly different from zero
options.D: The variances of the two groups are unequal

raw_questions_dump.explanation: A p-value less than 0.05 provides significant evidence to reject the null hypothesis of the Wilcoxon Signed-Rank Test, which states that the median of the differences between paired observations is zero.

questions.text:
Read the short passage (the ‘scenario’) that follows:

Passage:
A teacher sends an email to her class outlining the requirements for a group presentation. The email states that the students have to prepare a report on the assigned topic and bring a hard copy to the class on the following Monday. Students read the email and assume that ‘report’ meant ‘a brief summary’. They do not ask the teacher for clarification. On Monday, most students arrive with a short summary, while the teacher had expected a longer, more detailed report.

In this scenario which of the following options best describes the communication process that unfolded?
options: The teacher encodes the message, sends it via email, students decode it based on their assumption, but they fail to provide feedback which might have avoided the confusion that ensued.
The teacher sends a vague message, but the students decode it correctly, and the teacher receives the feedback on time.
The teacher sends an unclear message, the students encode a different interpretation and provides feedback to confirm their understanding.
The teacher uses informal language and a wrong channel of communication, and the student misinterprets the feedback leading to confusion.
questions.explanation: The breakdown occurred because the students decoded the term 'report' incorrectly based on their own assumptions and failed to use a feedback loop to clarify the requirements with the sender.

questions.text:
Read the statement given below. From the options provided, select the one that best completes the sentence.

Statement:
Spoken and written communication which is focused, reliable, and disciplined has the power to ______.
options: distract and confuse the audience
influence opinion and shape perception
decrease trust and credibility
limit the reach of the message
questions.explanation: Effective business communication is purposeful and designed to influence the audience's behavior and change their perceptions through shared understanding.

Statement:
In today's flat world, communication has emerged as arguably the most important source of competitive advantage.

questions.text:
Read the following passage (which is an excerpt from the novel Pride and Prejudice by Jane Austen) carefully.

Passage
Mr. Bingley was good enough to call at Longbourn, and Jane and Elizabeth were in the drawing room when he arrived. Mrs. Bennet was, as usual, anxious to make a good impression, and had prepared for him with a very fine breakfast. Mr. Bingley was as pleasant and agreeable as ever, and he and Jane exchanged pleasantries as they talked about the weather and the neighbourhood.

After some time, Mr. Bingley invited Jane to come and dine at Netherfield. Mrs. Bennet, ever eager to secure a match for her daughters, was thrilled at the idea and immediately accepted for Jane. Jane was delighted, though Elizabeth, a little wary, began to notice Mr. Bingley's attention to her sister.

The next day, Jane was indeed invited to Netherfield, and Mrs. Bennet insisted that Jane should stay overnight to avoid the long, tiring journey home. Jane was pleased, but Elizabeth, feeling protective of her sister, began to worry about the consequences of such an invitation.

Which of the options that follow best describes the manner in which the passage is structured? Select one.
options: Chronological
Spatial
Logical
Topical
questions.explanation: The passage follows a time-based sequence of events, utilizing phrases such as 'After some time' and 'The next day' to organize the narrative.

questions.text:
Read the following scenario carefully.

Scenario
A software company has received the following feedback from its clients:
1. Several clients have mentioned that the user interface of the software is difficult to navigate.
2. A significant number of customer support calls in the last month have been related to confusion over specific features of the software.
3. After implementing a minor redesign to make the interface more intuitive, customer support calls decreased by 30%. The company concludes that improving the user interface of the software leads to better customer understanding and fewer support issues. They decide to invest in User Interface improvements for other features of the software.

What is the logical approach the company has deployed in reaching a decision to invest in an improved User Interface?
options: An inductive approach
A deductive approach
A realistic approach
A factual approach to arrive at the decision
questions.explanation: Inductive reasoning involves moving from specific cases or evidence (individual feedback and a test redesign) to a generalized conclusion and decision.

With Best Regards,
Sindhu

Read the workplace scenario carefully and choose the best option given below to answer the question that follows appropriately.

In a workplace meeting, Sarita and Jagan are discussing a project deadline. Jagan feels that Sarita is not contributing enough and raises his voice, accusing her of not meeting expectations. Sarita, feeling attacked decides to not let Jagan get away with his accusations.

Question:
Which of the following behaviours is most likely to manifest in this situation due to the "fight" response from Sarita?
options: She calmly listen to Jagan's concerns and respond thoughtfully.
She becomes simultaneously aggressive and defensive.
She remains completely silent and avoids any eye contact.
Sarita engages in deep, reflective thinking before saying or doing anything.
questions.explanation: The 'fight or flight' response from the reptilian brain causes a reflexive reaction; if Sarita feels attacked, she is likely to retaliate with aggressive and defensive behavior.

questions.text: In a high-pressure meeting, your colleague makes a comment that frustrates you, and you feel the urge to retort immediately. What should you do to communicate more effectively?
options: Lash out to defend your point of view, ensuring your opinion is heard.
Take a deep breath, count to ten, and then respond calmly, considering all perspectives before reacting.
Stay silent and not respond, hoping the issue will resolve itself.
Interrupt your colleague to clarify your position right away, as it's important to correct any misunderstandings.
questions.explanation: Controlling the survival instinct involves a brief pause to allow the neocortex to process a rational response instead of an emotional reaction from the limbic brain.

Anil and Mala are work together on a project. Anil is well aware of his strengths and weaknesses and openly shares this with Mala. However, Mala is unsure about how much to disclose about herself and most often keeps her thoughts and feelings to herself.

According to the Johari Window Model, Anil’s and Mala’s communication profiles will be classified as _____________________________________ .
options: Arena and Facade respectively
Facade in both cases
Facade and Blind Spot respectively
Blind Spot and Arena respectively
questions.explanation: Anil’s open disclosure places him in the 'Arena' (known to self and others), while Mala’s reluctance to share keeps her in the 'Facade' (known to self, not to others).

questions.text: How can facilitators improve their leadership effectiveness during team meetings? Choose the best option given below to answer the question appropriately.

By actively guiding the conversation back on track when it goes off-topic and ensuring equal participation.

questions.text:
Read the following workplace scenario carefully and choose the best option given below to answer the question that follows appropriately:

You are a manager at a marketing firm. One of your team members, Arati, has been struggling with the quality of her presentations. Although she works hard and meets deadlines, her presentations often lack depth, and do not effectively communicate key points. You need to provide feedback on this issue.

Question:
Which of the following options deploys the Hamburger method for giving feedback?
options: "Arati, your presentations have been lacking in depth lately. The content is often too basic and doesn't meet the expected standard. This needs to be improved, or it could impact your future projects."

"Arati, your dedication to meeting deadlines is commendable, and I can see you put a lot of work into your presentations. However, your content lacks depth of the key details that we usually expect. I believe that, with more focus on refining the key points, you can enhance the quality of your work significantly, and I’m confident you’ll improve."

"Arati, I appreciate your punctuality with deadlines, and I know you're putting in effort. However, the presentations need to communicate more key insights, as they often lack depth. Putting in sincere efforts in building presentations will help you."
"Arati, you're doing a great job keeping up with the deadlines, but I think your presentations could benefit from a bit more detail. You have the potential to do this."
questions.explanation: The Hamburger method intersperses criticism with compliments: it starts with a positive lead-in, delivers the critical feedback, and ends with an encouraging note.

• What specific tools or strategies do you use when managing multiple projects simultaneously?

• Did you find it stressful?

questions.explanation: funnel. This technique starts with open-ended questions and gradually narrows down to more specific, probing, and closed questions.

questions.text:

The Statement:
One of the reasons why the 10-20-30 rule is effective in presentations is _______________________ .
options: It encourages excessive information to engage the audience, making the presentation more thorough.
It helps keep the presentation concise and focused, ensuring the audience remains engaged and the message is clear.
It allows the speaker to delve into finer details, which could be useful to the audience.
It forces the speaker to rush through information without sufficient explanation.
questions.explanation: The 10-20-30 rule prevents overloading the audience by limiting the number of slides and time, ensuring that only the most critical points are delivered clearly.

questions.text:
Read the passage carefully and follow the instruction given below:

The Passage Jaspreet, the project manager, is preparing a presentation for an internal team of software engineers relating to a new system upgrade his team is implementing. He understands that AIM Communication Model is key to the success of his presentation. He is planning how he might tailor his approach to ensure that his message resonates well with the software engineers in the team

The Instruction:
Choose the best option that includes the critical factors needed for aligning the audience, intent, and message effectively.
options: Ensuring the message is brief enough to allow the software engineers to interpret it in their own way, thereby fostering creativity and focusing their efforts.
Tailoring the depth of the content to match the software engineers' expertise, using clear, understandable language while emphasizing both the technical and business aspects of the upgrade.
Relying solely on visual aids and avoiding spoken content to ensure the software engineers stay focused on the slides, based on the concept that a picture is worth a thousand words.

Presenting as much technical details as possible in the shortest time to get the software engineers to understand the upgrade’s significance, and are able to meet the target and ensure success of the project.
questions.explanation: Aligning components of the AIM model requires a deep understanding of the audience to ensure the technical depth and messaging are appropriate for their specific role.

questions.text:

In a manufacturing company , there has been a growing concern about the company's high rate of employee turnover. Over the past year, approximately 25% of employees have left, resulting in significant disruption to project timelines, team morale, and overall productivity. Many employees are leaving for higher-paying opportunities, better work-life balance, and more engaging work environments in competing manufacturing companies.

The Instruction:
Choose the option from the four that follow which truly represents the structure : Cause –> Effect –> Solution.
options: Production is down 15 percent; the organization is experiencing a high rate of employee turnover; increase salaries and improve working conditions to retain employees
increase salaries and improve working conditions to retain employees; the organization is seeing a high rate of employee turnover; the Production is down 15 percent
the Production is down 15 percent; increase salaries and improve working conditions to retain employees; the organization is seeing a high rate of employee turnover
the organization is seeing a high rate of employee turnover; the Production is down 15 percent; increase salaries and improve working conditions to retain employees
questions.explanation: This sequence logicaly identifies the turnover as the primary cause, the production drop as the resulting effect, and wage increases as the solution.

making the presentation shorter, allowing you to skip over important details and focus only on the visuals.
questions.explanation: Infographics synergize text and visuals to create an impactful dual-channel experience that facilitates faster interpretation and better retention of data.

questions.text: Which of the following statements best explains the significance of the inverted pyramid approach in writing?

questions.text:
Sneha has been invited to give a presentation at her workplace. She is nervous because she has always struggled with Glossophobia.

Which one of the following options will NOT help Sneha to overcome her nervousness?
options: Rehearsing in front of a small, supportive audience before the actual presentation.
Focusing on deep breathing and mindfulness techniques.
Visualizing herself speaking confidently to an encouraging audience.
Focusing meticulously on getting every word or movement perfectly.
questions.explanation: Focusing on absolute perfection increases anxiety; it is better to focus on positive outcomes and connecting with friendly faces.

questions.text:

Choose the option that best explains the reason for deploying the 10-20-30 Rule as a good strategy for the investor pitch presentation.
options: It forces you to focus on the most important points, delivering them concisely, and keeping the audience engaged within the time limit.
It helps you include all the technical aspects of your startup, regardless of how long it takes to explain each one.
It gives you enough time to present the detailed financial analysis and risk assessments that investors require.
It ensures that the presentation is visually appealing by focusing on design rather than content.
questions.explanation: The 10-20-30 rule (10 slides, 20 minutes, 30 point font) ensures the presentation remains focused on essential insights within a limited timeframe.

raw_questions_dump.question_text:
Read the short passage (the ‘scenario’) that follows:

Passage:
A teacher sends an email to her class outlining the requirements for a group presentation. The email states that the students have to prepare a report on the assigned topic and bring a hard copy to the class on the following Monday. Students read the email and assume that ‘report’ meant ‘a brief summary’. They do not ask the teacher for clarification. On Monday, most students arrive with a short summary, while the teacher had expected a longer, more detailed report.

In this scenario which of the following options best describes the communication process that unfolded?

options.A: The teacher encodes the message, sends it via email, students decode it based on their assumption, but they fail to provide feedback which might have avoided the confusion that ensued.
options.B: The teacher sends a vague message, but the students decode it correctly, and the teacher receives the feedback on time.
options.C: The teacher sends an unclear message, the students encode a different interpretation and provides feedback to confirm their understanding.
options.D: The teacher uses informal language and a wrong channel of communication, and the student misinterprets the feedback leading to confusion.

raw_questions_dump.explanation: The breakdown occurred because the students decoded the term 'report' incorrectly based on their own assumptions and failed to use a feedback loop to clarify the requirements with the sender.
